programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina begins an out-of-this-world romance with a ghost that is believed to be living in the Belcher's basement.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers

programme.title: MasterChef Canada

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny considers running for town council. David and Stevie look for love. Alexis copes with being single.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother

Robin asks Mary lots of questions, and Ted begins to really like Mary, despite her occupation.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: When Jake repeats to Judith something Charlie said, she refuses to let Alan take Jake for the weekend. Alan tries to talk to Judith, but encounters resistance from her and her friends. It's up to Charlie to set things right.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is sure he isn't ready for fatherhood after a year of trying to raise Jake, so he opts to have a vasectomy. But when the doctor performing the procedure keeps getting calls from his wife, who's about to give birth, Charlie starts to think about what fatherhood might entail and likes the idea, provided he still has his vital organs intact... in more ways than one.

Jack learns that one of the assassins, Alexis Mecir, is actually Victor Drazen's son and has become intimate with one of Palmer's aides. In an attempt to find the man ordering the assassinations, the aide agrees to plant a tracking device in Alexis' wallet. Back at the safe house, Teri and Kim discover their location is no longer secret or safe.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.sub-title: There But For the Grace of God

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Princess Leia Fantasy
programme.desc: Ross reveals a fantasy to Rachel involving a certain "Star Wars" character. Monica suffers from insomnia after her breakup with Richard. And Chandler, back together with Janice, wants Joey to bond with her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where No One's Ready
programme.desc: In a race against the clock, a stressed Ross tries to get the gang dressed and ready for a black-tie benefit at the museum while Joey and Chandler clash over personal belongings and personal space.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe dates a man who thinks he's stalking her twin sister. Ross and Rachel give Chandler "boyfriend" lessons. Monica decides the best way to get over Richard is to keep herself busy, so she uses her free time to make jam and considers insemination via a sperm bank.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: While Agent Scully is still missing, Agent Mulder embarks upon an investigation involving vampires in Los Angeles, subsequently becoming involved with a sultry female vampire who yearns to escape her lifestyle.

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: On Halloween, Buffy and her friends patronize an unusual costume shop where customers turn into whatever costume they are wearing.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam and Diane want to spend some time alone together, but the lonely Coach keeps joining them, not realising he is spoiling things for them. programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff's know-it-all attitude finally gets him into a fight with another patron.

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.desc:
This year, Angie decides that her and George should just get something cheap and from the heart for their anniversary. This gives Vic an opening to convince George to help him smuggle his brother Ontovio out of Cuba.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.sub-title: Would You Like a Drumstick or a Kidney?
programme.desc: After inviting his extended family to Thanksgiving dinner, George learns his estranged father needs a new kidney.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.desc: George gets rid of some of Angie's cherished mementos as he prepares to donate a kidney.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.desc: At Christmastime, George will stop at nothing to convince Max to continue believing in Santa Claus.

programme.desc: After Martin bemoans his sons' rarefied tastes and avoidance of all that is ordinary, they try to prove him wrong by fixing the toilet; they make the problem worse, call in a plumber, and find that they've hired Danny Kriezel (John McGinley), a bully who tormented Niles long ago.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a London couple find a perfect rural escape in Cornwall that would enable them to live the 'good life'. On a budget of £400,000, can Jules deliver? Away from the property hunt, Jules explores Cornwall's most southerly tip.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Boyle go on a stakeout and tensions rise between the two, Rosa shows interest in Holt's nephew, and Terry writes a book for his daughters. Santiago and Gina are displeased when they assume the characters in the book are based on them.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchell tries to tell Cameron how he feels uncomfortable with his mom, Barb Tucker. Meanwhile, Haley breaks up with Dylan much to Phil's dismay and Claire's delight. Jay has a little stomach ache that gets exacerbated when he and Manny look it up on the Internet.

programme.desc: At Luke and Manny's first school dance, Jay and Phil take them to the mall which turns disastrous, Claire's happiness turns into jealousy when Gloria joins the school dance committee. Meanwhile, Cameron and Mitchell learn about Lily biting other kids at the playground.

programme.desc: After Ron breaks up with Tom's ex-wife, he decides to get back together with his Tammy, much to everyone's horror. Meanwhile, Leslie and Ben try to get the local police to donate their services to the upcoming Harvest Festival.

programme.desc: Leslie hits the Pawnee media circuit to promote the Harvest Festival, but Ben freaks out on air when his past is exposed. Meanwhile, April threatens Andy that she'll leave for Indianapolis unless he does her chores.

programme.desc: Moira is worried that racy photos from her past will jeopardize the campaign; Johnny asks for financial help; Alexis gets a job.

programme.desc: It's Ted's 28th birthday and he still keeps looking for the perfect girl. programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is having some friends over to his house for a gathering but Alan is not invited. Things get worse for Alan when he is also not invited to Judith's party even though her guests are Alan's friends.

programme.desc: Judith is dating Jake's pediatrician, and Alan is upset until Charlie points out that if she remarries, his alimony payments will end.

programme.desc: Senator Kinsey arrives at the SGC to investigate the program and determine whether the great drain on the U.S. budget is worthwhile, prompting the team to recall missions from the past year.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe has trouble bonding with her half-brother Frank when he comes to visit. Joey attempts to build an entertainment center. Ross is challenged to name the five celebrities he'd like to sleep with most.

programme.sub-title: Late Afternoon in the Garden of Bob and Louise

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina teams up with Darryl to win the Cupid's Couple contest at the school dance. Meanwhile, Linda plans a full week of ambitious Valentine's Day activities for her and Bob.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina and Louise volunteer to run Jimmy Junior's class president campaign in order to keep Millie from winning, and ruining the school. Meanwhile, Bob becomes obsessed with a knife after talking himself into buying it for $300.

programme.title: Joe Pera: Slow & Steady

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira’s campaign takes a turn when some of her lawn signs mysteriously disappear. David and Alexis must work together to get David’s boss out of legal trouble.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: The gang tries to help Marshall get over Lily, especially when he finds a credit card bill that leads to a hotel in New York City where some of her charges are listed.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Barney works with Marshall on his bachelor skills, which include hitting on women. Lily is back from her venture. Robin helps her look for a new apartment but gets sick of hearing about San Francisco.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is dating a self-assured woman who plays hard to get, easily dismisses him, and he finds it disarming. Rose gets Charlie to see that his new lady-friend, Sherry, is in his head because she is just like him; a self-centered, manipulative, narcissist. Charlie debates breaking it off. Alan and Jake fiercely battle wills over Jake doing his homework.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: The Price of Healthy Gums Is Eternal Vigilance
programme.desc: Alan denies having shoplifted a toy as a child and fears he has a dark side, until Charlie admits he planted the toy on Alan to stay out of trouble himself.

Alexis' contact turns out to be a power company worker who was being paid to shut off the electricity to a power grid out in the middle of an empty field. As Jack and Mason follow up on that lead, Kim's search for information lands her in jail, even as Palmer's press conference disclosure could ultimately put his own son in jail.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: With SG-1 trapped on Klorel's ship, it seems that Earth is doomed as the Pyramid Ships prepare to destroy Earth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel makes a rash decision after Gunther tells her she needs to be retrained. Phoebe tries to help Joey when he gets a job selling Christmas trees. And Ross accidentally breaks a girl's leg and tries to make it up to her.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister
programme.desc: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger. And a drunken Chandler fools around with one of Joey's sisters, but he can't remember which one.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Jealousy
programme.desc: Ross becomes increasingly jealous of Rachel's new colleague, Mark, and spies on her. Monica hooks up with a co-worker, who writes her a poem with a hidden meaning. One of Joey's numerous lies on his resume is exposed when he takes part in a Broadway musical.

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Spike brings in three professional bounty hunters to eliminate Buffy while she ponders on what her profession might be if her fate wasn't already sealed.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam commissions a portrait of Diane from an artist so obnoxious that he soon forbids Diane to continue posing for him, an order Diane won't accept.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam commissions a portrait of Diane from an artist so obnoxious that he soon forbids Diane to continue posing for him, an order Diane won't accept.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam falls off the wagon and Diane has a nervous breakdown after the breakup. But after she's cured, Diane returns to Cheers to recommend her psychiatrist--Dr. Frasier Crane--to help Sam. But she doesn't say that she's involved with him.

programme.title: The Nanny
programme.desc: Fran has to have her tonsils removed, so Maxwell puts C.C.'s reunion on hold to await the outcome of Fran's surgery.

programme.title: The Nanny
programme.desc: On the way to catch a flight to the islands, heavy snow maroons the family at Fran's parents' home in Queens.

programme.title: 3rd Rock from the Sun
programme.desc: Dick receives a roundabout lesson in family values from Dr. Albright's loopy brother, who claims to have been abducted by aliens.

programme.title: 3rd Rock from the Sun
programme.desc: When she can't go to a meeting where she has to speak, Dr. Albright flatters Dick in order to get him to go the meeting for her. After Dick discovers the trick behind the flattering he doesn't understand why people lie to each other. Sally and Harry are trying to get themselves a driver's license but discover it's not that easy to get one. Meanwhile, Tommy is upset because he has a pimple.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: The Show Where Sam Shows Up
programme.desc: Sam Malone visits Frasier in Seattle; it turns out he needs advice on whether to go through with his wedding. Complications arise when Frasier realizes that he had an affair with Sam's fiancée, Sheila.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier retrieves a book from Daphne's room while she's out, and she's displeased when she finds out. Although he agrees to respect her privacy, he finds himself there again, under more complicated circumstances.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier and Niles put themselves forward as candidates for two vacant memberships in Seattle's exclusive Empire Club; upon learning that there is only one vacancy, they try to sabotage each other at the opening cocktail party.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen (US)

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen (US)
programme.desc: A shocking elimination forces both teams to regroup and chart a new course as they take on the Seven Seas Seafood Challenge, which gives the chefs 30 minutes to prepare a fish dish using a variety of fresh ingredients. Later, guitarist Joe Perry and singer Estelle are invited to the dinner service.

programme.title: Kitchen Nightmares (US)
programme.desc: The Sante la Brea in Hollywood CA is run by a manager who can't manage and his two sons; one who needs guidance and the other who just doesn't seem to listen, on top of a cook in low gear and a kitchen that causes a shut down.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: It's the day of Gina and Charles' parents' wedding, and the whole squad is given a job to fulfill for the ceremony. However, Jake and Amy get sidetracked chasing a criminal, and Terry struggles to officiate the wedding.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake's mainly absentee father, Roger (guest star Bradley Whitford), comes to town to spend some time with his son. Jake can't wait to see him, but Charles is skeptical of Roger's intentions. Meanwhile, Holt tasks Amy, Terry, Gina and Rosa with an impossible brain teaser in exchange for Beyoncé tickets.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The guest list for Lily's princess-themed birthday party gets a little out of hand when Mitchell decides to invite his mom, much to Claire's dismay, and disinvite Fizbo, much to Cameron's dismay. Things get even more strange when DeDe shows up with Claire's old high school boyfriend in tow, and Jay and Gloria each turn to their own unique methods of dealing with this gathering of misfits.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Claire have a huge fight and Phil hasn't a clue as to what it was over, so Gloria steps in to counsel Phil while Jay counsels Claire. Meanwhile, Jay has some regret in buying Gloria a karaoke machine, and Cameron is in full prep mode for a huge fund-raising event and asks Mitchell and Luke for help.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Ann convinces Leslie to sign up for an online dating service, only to be matched with someone she already knows. Chris decides to start a health initiative and challenges Ron to a burger cook-off.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Jerry’s contribution to Leslie’s community art show causes a stir. Ben moves in with April and Andy and attempts to teach them how to be adults. Meanwhile, Leslie is frustrated by Chris and his interoffice dating rules.

programme.title: Monk
programme.desc: When a rash of murders sweeping the city goes unsolved, Monk is called upon to find a correlation with the victims and stop a killer before he strikes again.

programme.title: Monk
programme.desc: When his paperboy is murdered, Monk turns to the pages of the newspaper for clues to solve the baffling crime.

programme.title: Monk
programme.sub-title: Mr. Monk and the Three Pies
programme.desc: Monk begins his day at the scene of a carjacking where an elderly woman was killed. Monk suspects that all is not as it seems. He then receives a call from his estranged brother who is an agoraphobic and won't leave the house. Monk must mend fences with his brother and solve the mystery of why his brother's mysterious neighbor seems obsessed with winning some cherry pies in the local fair.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Gayle arrives at Bob's Burgers in tears because her date for the evening was cancelled and she had theater tickets. Desperate to get out of the house after being grounded, the kids compete to be Aunt Gayle's "date" for the evening via an essay contest.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Bob becomes obsessed with his new automatic helicopter. When it falls apart for no reason, he enters into a battle with the manufacturer in order to receive a refund. Gene assists Bob in his quest, but things quickly get out of hand and end in a crazy helicopter battle. Meanwhile, Louise and Linda help Tina prepare for her oral book report.

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Bob and Linda head out on the town for a date - but to Linda's dismay, Bob's idea of romance proves "trivial." Meanwhile, a wild chinchilla chase ensues after the school pet that Louise was charged with watching escapes out the front door.

programme.title: Superbad
programme.desc: Two co-dependent high school seniors are forced to deal with separation anxiety after their plan to stage a booze-soaked party goes awry.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira go out for their anniversary and run into a couple they knew from their former life, while Alexis and David attend Mutt’s annual barn party, making up with some friends, and making out with others.

programme.title: Arrested Development
programme.desc: Michael cannot go through with his plan to break up Gob and Marta. A thug magician wants to take Gob's "legs". Maeby wears all leather to get back at her mother, and Lucille decides to confront Buster's relationship with Lucille 2.

programme.title: Arrested Development
programme.desc: Buster realizes that medical marijuana will help cure Lucille 2's vertigo, and pays George Michael to get it for him from Gob. Lindsay sends Maeby to spend a day with Lucille to punish her for bad grades. Michael thinks George Michael is on drugs and attempts to teach him a lesson.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Refusing to accept that their crazy days are behind them, Jess and Cece abandon their low-key bachelorette party plans and seek a night out to top all others.

programme.desc: When Marshall and Barney argue over the destruction of the Arcadian Hotel, Robin and Lily try to mix the right combination of cocktails that will force them to make up. Meanwhile, Ted plans a romantic weekend away with Zoey.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Holt and Jake become suspicious of a new Nine-Nine employee. Amy goes full-on Marie Kondo to declutter the bullpen.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon has a revelation when he realizes why a song was stuck in his head. Also, Amy invites Dave over for dinner despite their previously awkward date, and Wolowitz and Koothrappali become obsessed with a fan of their band, "Footprints on the Moon".

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Cheap Wedding Dress

programme.title: Archer (2009)
programme.desc: Archer is convinced a mysterious object is causing the crew to act strangely. Is it magnetic? Is it supernatural? Does it need to be cleaned with some sterile wipes?

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael decides to propose to Holly.

programme.title: Community
programme.desc: A plan to celebrate Jeff's early graduation is unexpectedly disrupted by the study group's evil doppelgangers from the Darkest Timeline.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys ask strangers for help with big items at a grocery store and give some entrepreneurial tips to some unfortunate souls. Tonight's big loser strikes out at a little league softball game while being "that dad."

programme.title: Team America: World Police

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
programme.desc: It’s Valentine’s Day and Paddy’s is packed with customers. Eventually, the gang becomes convinced Dennis really wants them to work on their relationships and everyone shares their grievances without much resolution.

programme.title: George Lopez

programme.title: Friends

Also, Monica freaks out a little when she realizes that after marrying Chandler she's not gonna experience a rush of dating someone new ever again. In a surprising change of roles, Chandler becomes a voice of reason that calms her.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: When Ted is faced with an important decision that might affect his future with Zoey, the gang expresses their true feelings about her.

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Frankie and Mike find themselves caught in the middle of a long standing family feud when Frankie's Uncle Dutch contacts her dad, Tag in an attempt to reunite and bury the hatchet with his estranged brother. Meanwhile, a fearful Sue receives her first detention ever for being caught going off campus for lunch and seeks advice from Axl on how to survive the ordeal unscathed.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver breaks down the real scenario behind the carbon offsets. He detailed out that how the loopholes are in this process and what little impact it has in the environment. Finally he himself opened a carbon registry company to show easy to open one and what little effort it takes it to run it with such low standards.

programme.title: The Office (US)

programme.desc: The "TGS" crew anticipate their summer vacation. Liz lands in the Hamptons for some rest and relaxation, but she gets the summertime blues when Tracy moves in next door. Elsewhere, a lonely Jack latches onto Kenneth for company; and Jenna's new job impacts her relationship with Paul.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)
programme.desc: A big party is being thrown at Dave Kim's house and Pops has a plan to make Adam cool for the big event. Meanwhile, Beverly's cookbook has made her "famous," leading her to become out of control and fame-hungry, but eventually she learns an important lesson.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)

programme.title: Everybody Hates Chris

programme.title: Archer (2009)
programme.desc: When a trial turns into a party it can only mean that Barry-6 has returned!

programme.title: Clerks II
programme.desc: A calamity at Dante and Randal's shops sends them looking for new horizons, but they ultimately settle at Mooby's, a fictional fast-food restaurant. Free from his dead-end job and lodged in a new one, Dante begins to break free of his rut, planning to move away with his clingy fiancée. He is ready to leave the horrors of minimum-wage New Jersey behind, but Randal, always the more hostile of the two, starts to become overwhelmed by his own rancor.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver discusses the wildly popular television franchise, what it’s been teaching us about law enforcement, and how it differs from the reality. Plus, some important developments in British politics.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
programme.desc: We get to see a typical day in Cricket's world and then a possible redemption when his family intervenes. Will Cricket finally move on from the gang, finding love in the process? Will Cricket go from filthy beast to his former self, Matthew Mara?

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Ted and Zoey's break-up, Ted is having second thoughts. He is freaking out about an issue with the GNB headquarters, and historically when Ted freaks out about something, he turns to dating an old girlfriend if only for something comfortable and familiar.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Amy and Jake hear conflicting stories as they investigate a sexual assault accusation. Holt refuses to believe an old nemesis is really dead.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Jess races to make alterations to Cece’s bridal dress, while Schmidt tries to keep his “wedding workshop” a secret at work, and Nick struggles to ask Reagan to be his date to the nuptials. Meanwhile, Winston and Aly try to keep their relationship on the down-low at work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Ross and Monica's Cousin
programme.desc: Joey auditions for a big movie role which will require him to appear naked. A problem arises, however, when the part calls for an uncircumcised man. Meanwhile, Rachel and Phoebe go through the trouble of organizing a last minute bridal shower for Monica. And Ross becomes attracted to his cousin after she visits.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys shock shoppers by going footloose and fancy free in a discount score. Then they pose insane questions to strangers in a park. Tonight's big loser is taken for a wild ride in a decked out wheelchair that he can't control.

programme.title: Utopia (AU)
programme.desc: A routine urban development project proves surprisingly difficult for Tony when multiple Government departments get involved. Nat is forced to assist the Minister with a major challenge - choosing this year's Christmas cards.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver

Plus, the absurd stunt by Florida's Governor Ron DeSantis involving Martha's Vineyard and why people in Puerto Rico are getting fed up by the electric company Luma.

programme.title: Community
programme.desc: Having failed in his second go-round as a lawyer, and unable to gather evidence for a potential lawsuit against Greendale, Jeff tries to convince the other former members of the Study Group to sue the school.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Michael trains Deangelo to take over the job of hosting the Dundie Awards.

programme.title: George Lopez

programme.desc: After having to deal with a sick Sheldon, Leonard, Penny and the gang try to treat themselves to a Sheldon-free weekend.

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Frankie and Mike struggle to stay awake when watching a movie during a cozy date night at home. Meanwhile, a handsome mall employee asks Sue to her senior prom and Axl and Brick hope to find success with a barbecue sauce they've created.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie wins an award from an international Women in Government organization, and travels to London with Ben, Andy, April and Ron to accept it.

programme.title: 30 Rock
programme.desc: Everybody becomes confused when Liz suddenly becomes cheerful, Jack tries to talk Jenna out of being the mean judge in a children's reality talent show, and Kenneth prepares for what he believes to be the imminent Rapture.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia

In the end, Dennis has to decide which life he wants to continue to lead.

programme.title: The Middle

programme.title: Idiocracy

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Rachel's Big Kiss

Meanwhile, Monica plans the seating arrangement for the wedding and is taken by surprise when Joey mentions that his parents are coming to the wedding too. Also, Chandler and Ross argue who gets to wear a celebrity tux for the event.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: While Barney prepares for his wedding to a mystery bride, he and Ted reminisce about Punchy’s wedding, including when Marshall and Lily announced they are having a baby. Meanwhile, Robin considers telling Barney she has feelings for him.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver discusses some of the world's most prestigious museums, why they contain so many stolen goods, the market that continues to illegally trade antiquities, and a pretty solid blueprint for revenge. Plus, the disappearance of the British PM Liz Truss.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is thrilled when his Meemaw comes to visit, but his excitement quickly fades when she butts heads with Amy. Meanwhile, Raj meets a girl at the comic book store who makes him question his relationship with Emily.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake helps Amy navigate the complicated relationship she has with her brother, David. Charles introduces some drama into an undercover mission involving Terry and Holt.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler are getting married in four weeks and have to write their vows, but neither has a clue what to say, so they reflect on their past. In the end, the only real problem is who will be saying the vows first.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Jess meets Sam’s female best friend, Diane. Meanwhile, Nick is worried Gavin will disappoint Schmidt again and Winston is offended by a reaction to a birthday gift.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: As the office gets ready for Michael's final day at Dunder Mifflin, Michael doesn't tell anyone that he's leaving a day early. Meanwhile, Deangelo accompanies a nervous Andy on a visit to one of Michael's old clients.

programme.title: The Great North
programme.desc: Ham enlists his family for help in finding a speech for his Public Speaking Elective. Judy, Beef, and Honeybee pitch competing stories for Ham to select from and end up secretly drawing from Top Gun, Good Will Hunting, and The Matrix in the process.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Marshall gets a great job offer that he fears may be scuttled by a social media background check. Ted needs a date for the Architects Ball.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver discusses the outlets that cover crime, the incentives that drive them, the flawed sources they rely upon, and an alternative name for Miss Piggy. Plus, why teenage girls in Iran are leading massive protests against the current regime.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)
programme.desc: Mother and son have a bonding experience when Beverly buys a bicycle for Adam just like the one from “Pee Wee’s Big Adventure,” while Barry and Erica turn Murray’s furniture store into an after-hours lounge for college students.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.sub-title: George Says I Do ... More in This Marriage
programme.desc: While giving marriage counseling to a young engaged couple from their church, George and Angie discover that they disagree about who works harder in their own marriage. They set up a point system based on their household tasks and compete for dominance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys make fools of each other as restaurant managers, name that (made up) tune with strangers and then head to the park for some awkward fun in the sun. Tonight's big loser gets wrapped up in a birthday surprise.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
programme.sub-title: The Gang Makes Paddy's Great Again
programme.desc: Meet Cindy, the new, ethnically diverse female member of the gang who can successfully pull off scams. Everything is working until Mac introduces a new member: a life-like sex doll that looks exactly like Dennis.

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Sue prepares for her graduation and worries the day won't go perfectly. Meanwhile, Brick gets the opportunity to skip a year in school and Axl grows concerned about Devin's fun summer pictures she posted on social media.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything
programme.desc: Adam Conover reveals that the US kicked off the Cuban Missile Crisis, and that you've probably never heard of the lone female politician who stood up to McCarthy! Plus, a bureaucratic mistake brought down the Berlin Wall.

programme.title: Everybody Hates Chris
programme.desc: As a first-time high school student, Chris (Tyler James Williams) is really looking forward to attending a school with some diversity. Unfortunately, when he arrives at school, he discovers that the school's new principal, Ms. Morello (guest star Jacqueline Mazarella), has placed him in the only all-white homeroom.

programme.title: Borat
programme.desc: Kazakh journalist Borat Sagdiyev travels to America to make a documentary. As he zigzags across the nation, Borat meets real people in real situations with hysterical consequences. His backwards behavior generates strong reactions around him exposing prejudices and hypocrisies in American culture.

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Employees wrestle with the loss of beloved co-worker Myrtle… and the fact that she left a large sum of money to Jonah. Cheyenne recruits Mateo in a possible pyramid scheme, while Dina reconnects with a surprise visitor in the store: her father.

programme.title: Duncanville
programme.desc: Duncan gets a knife and becomes the alpha of his friend group. Meanwhile, Jack and Annie discover they can sell their clothes online.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver discusses the latest round of attacks on transgender rights, what’s at stake, and how kids are schooling the ignorant adults on this topic. Plus, why the Crimean Bridge is having a bad time these days.

programme.title: Community
programme.desc: Jeff blows off his new teaching job at Greendale until Annie signs up for his class and forces him to teach, and Abed loses his religion when he takes a course about whether Nicolas Cage is good or bad.

programme.title: 30 Rock
programme.desc: Liz tries to keep a judgmental Jack away from her new boyfriend, Tracy begins an "idiot protest" in response to public comments made by Liz, and Kenneth and Jenna accidentally break a box of fluorescent lights in the storage closet.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: With the U.S. midterm elections approaching, John Oliver discusses the issue at the core of many republican attack ads: bail reform. Plus, the turmoil in British politics after the resignation of PM Liz Truss.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Monica's Porsche shows up again, Ross and Rachel hit the freeway together and it turns out to be an adventurous ride. Meanwhile, Chandler and Monica go off to Vegas to find Chandler's estranged father. And back at home, Joey proves his masculinity to Phoebe.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When the gang finds out about Bernadette’s pregnancy, they celebrate by singing karaoke. Also, Bernadette is concerned when Wolowitz freaks out over her pregnancy announcement, and Wolowitz is convinced he must make more money to provide for the baby.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie pairs up the Pawnee Parks employees with their Eagleton counterparts. Meanwhile Ben and Chris reunite for an accounting project, and Ann reveals her plans for the future.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)
programme.desc: Erica, Barry, Geoff and the JTP go on a spring break trip and things are not what they expect. Meanwhile, Adam questions if college is for him when his test scores are not as good as he was hoping for.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: When Deangelo plays favorites, his management style is exposed.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Barney faces having to wear Marshall's ducky tie if he loses a bet with him. Meanwhile, Ted hopes to apologize to an ex for cheating on her.

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Sue's excitement about moving into the dorm to start college is tempered when she accidentally burns her hair and must cut it short. Axl isn't interested in helping with Sue's move. Frankie discovers she's not sad about Sue moving out of the house while Mike overwhelms Sue with financial and safety guidelines. Brick is clueless when Cindy tells him she wants to take their relationship to the next level.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: On the night before the wedding, Nick and Winston try to help Schmidt re-write his vows and Jess, thinking that Sam is going to propose to her, rallies the gang into playing True American to avoid giving him an answer.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Formica King
programme.desc: Business rival and nemesis "Formica" Mike attempts to make an offer Murray can't refuse by buying Ottoman Empire. Meanwhile, Adam offers Ms. Cinoman his and Erica's original show when William Penn cannot secure the rights to a well-known spring musical to stage.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: A visitor from Latvia causes Charles and Jake to investigate Nikolaj's family. Holt and Amy bring in famed forensic scientist Dr. Yee to help close a case for Rosa.

programme.title: The Ricky Gervais Show
programme.desc: Ricky, Stephen and Karl discuss what things Karl would banish from the world, and what item he would take if stranded on an island.

programme.title: Archer (2009)
programme.desc: Archer and the gang attend a martial arts tournament to capture an international arms dealer.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: With the U.S. midterm elections approaching, John Oliver discusses what happens after the votes are in, how some elected officials might try to negate legitimate election results, and which teletubby would taste best. Plus, the Halloween costume of Heidi Klum.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After more than nine years together, the gang finally celebrates Sheldon’s birthday, surprising him with a special guest.

programme.title: Tenacious D in the Pick of Destiny
programme.desc: To become the greatest band of all time, two slackers, wannabe-rockers set out on a quest to steal a legendary guitar pick that gives its holders incredible guitar skills, from a maximum security Rock and Roll museum.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Chandler and Monica's Wedding
programme.desc: So close to the wedding, Chandler suddenly realizes the overwhelming meaning of getting married. Meanwhile, Joey acts opposite a famous actor in a movie, but can't bring himself to tell him that he spits when he says his lines.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: After Robin assaults a girl who is out to ruin Barney's relationship, she is forced to begin court-mandated therapy sessions. Meanwhile, Ted gets too involved in Lily's pregnancy.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
programme.desc: To boost productivity, Dee hires an at-home "Escape Room Experience" service to come to Dennis and Mac's apartment. Will they escape? Who will get the credit? What could go wrong?

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys give an eye opening experience at a glasses store and convince grocery store shoppers to pass ridiculous messages while shopping. Tonight's big loser gives back at a Career Day assembly that's divorced from reality.

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy tries to make Mateo her new assistant, leading to accusations of favoritism and a group competition in the store. Sandra gets caught in a power struggle between Glenn and Dina, and Jonah attempts to show Garrett that he's still one of the guys.

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Anxiously awaiting the arrival of her dorm roommate, Sue drives Frankie and Mike crazy with multiple calls and texts. When the roommate shows up Sue thinks she'll make a life- long friend and all her worries will be gone. Axl and Hutch find their house overrun by ants. Brick gets his own personal bodyguard at school.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.sub-title: George is Being Elfish and Christ-misses His Family
programme.desc: George ends up spending Christmas all by himself after he gets caught telling a lie to get out of spending the holiday with Angie's family.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Sheldon, and Wolowitz run into problems when they file for a patent for their infinite persistence gyroscope. Also, Penny, Amy and Bernadette give Koothrappali dating advice when Emily reaches out to him after their breakup.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Dwight takes charge of the office and immediately imposes his will on the staff. Gabe hopes to get back together with Erin.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver discusses the future of the British monarchy, what they have and have not acknowledged about their past, and how Winston Churchill preferred to go down waterslides. Plus, Steve Kornacki and his big board.

programme.title: 30 Rock
programme.desc: Tracy challenges Liz by leading his own protest in defense of idiots everywhere. Jack faces off with Devin Banks yet again, and the Best Friends Gang is reunited when Kenneth and Jenna call for the help of Kelsey Grammer.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver discusses the Qatar World Cup, the human-rights violations happening in the sporting event’s host country, and what perfection really means to David Beckham. Plus, the chaos Twitter is experiencing after Elon Musk took over the company and more of AI-generated art featuring John Oliver.

programme.title: The Great North
programme.desc: Beef has a one-night stand after a string of bad dates, but quickly becomes embarrassed when Greta Meatweep exposes their sexcapades in the poem column of the local newspaper. Moon becomes Jerry’s Bigfoot little-brother sidekick, and the local clown duo challenges them to a costume battle over the Lone Moose party turf.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)
programme.desc: While throwing a telethon at their college to raise money for Earth Day, Barry attempts to woo Ren when he overpromises he can get Hall & Oates to perform. Meanwhile, Beverly attempts to set Bill up, but things go awry when he finds happiness with a woman he meets through Beverly's enemy

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie tries to calm a minor scandal that gets blown out of proportion. Tom tries to impress a new love interest, while Ben convinces Ron to prepare a will.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
programme.sub-title: The Gang Beats Boggs: Ladies Reboot
programme.desc: To one-up the guys, Dee has organized an "all-female reboot" of the Wade Boggs challenge on a first-of-its-kind, all-female flight headed to L.A. for the women's march.

programme.title: Everybody Hates Chris
programme.desc: Chris agrees to help a classmate, Angel improve his grades if Angel will help him get the attention of Chris' latest crush, Maria.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: Robin tries to date her therapist, Marshall thinks his boss has gone soft on environmentalism, Barney suspects that Nora may be lying about her age, and Ted takes his Intro to Architecture class on the worst field trip ever.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: The excitement of Monica and Chandler's wedding is diminished by the news that someone may be pregnant. Chandler's hopes of not embarrassing Monica on the dance floor are ruined by his slippery new shoes, and Ross' attempts to flirt with an attractive wedding guest.

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Frankie is shocked when Mike trades in his flannel for a Hawaiian shirt. Sue accidentally tells Devin that Axl loves her, leading Devin to question their relationship. Brick gets overambitious while cleaning the bathroom.

programme.title: Hot Fuzz
programme.desc: Former London constable Nicholas Angel finds it difficult to adapt to his new assignment in the sleepy British village of Sandford. Not only does he miss the excitement of the big city, but he also has a well-meaning oaf for a partner. However, when a series of grisly accidents rocks Sandford, Angel smells something rotten in the idyllic village.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: John Oliver discusses psychedelic assisted therapy: its history, its potential, and what it has to do with A$AP Rocky’s relationship to rainbows. Plus, the Fox News lawsuit and the Ohio train derailment.

programme.title: Community
programme.desc: With the mysterious Ass Crack Bandit back at Greendale, Annie and Jeff are on the case. As clues and suspicions mount, Shirley delivers shocking news.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Charles gets Jake involved in a case where a therapist reports one of his patients missing; Holt learns that Rosa has a new girlfriend whom everyone else has met; Amy thinks she mistakenly received a package for Terry.

programme.title: How I Met Your Mother
programme.desc: When Ted goes on a date without researching the girl on the Internet, the gang interferes, showing Kevin that they are too into each other's business. Meanwhile, Lily and Marshall come to terms with finding out the sex of their baby.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)
programme.desc: Adam and Brea's relationship is going well until Beverly's over-involvement causes them to fake a breakup. Meanwhile, Lainey is back in Jenkintown which throws Barry for a loop regarding his blossoming relationship with Ren.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is shocked at a revelation from Sheldon after she buys him a new laptop. Also, Koothrappali sells out Leonard and Wolowitz after they lie to their wives in order to attend an early screening of a movie.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: The Scranton branch puts together a search committee to find the next boss, but the pool of interviewees proves to be less than impressive, and Dwight demands an interview in spite of his gun accident.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Schmidt makes a last ditch effort to convince Cece's mom to come to their wedding, but in doing so, he might miss the special day himself.

programme.title: Archer (2009)
programme.desc: The Agency attempts to steal experimental technology from a reclusive inventor, but they forgot one vital item... Sterling Archer.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Red Sweater
programme.desc: Speculation regarding the father of Rachel's baby leads to a misunderstanding when Joey discovers a red sweater left behind in a one-night stand Rachel had. Meanwhile, Chandler and Ross search for the disposable cameras left behind at the wedding and Monica goes on a present-opening binge.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon spills a secret of Leonard’s when the two take a weekend cabin trip with Penny and Amy. Also, when Koothrappali goes overboard with pregnancy gifts, Wolowitz and Bernadette must draw the line.

programme.title: George Lopez
programme.desc: When Max skips school, George enrolls in college to set a positive example. But George has difficulty getting anyone to take him seriously. Even Professor Lim (recurring guest star MING-NA - "ER") thinks his questions are jokes.

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Sandra and Jerry are getting married! When Jonah’s attempt to help the caterers and Dina’s obsession with wedding security threaten to derail it all, it falls to Amy to make sure Sandra’s big day isn’t ruined. Garrett struggles with what to say in his Best Man toast, while Mateo finds that starting a new relationship may be more complicated than he thought.

programme.title: Last Week Tonight with John Oliver
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is increasingly becoming part of our lives, from self-driving cars to ChatGPT. John Oliver discusses how AI works, where it might be heading next, and, of course, why it hates the bus.

programme.title: Adam Ruins Everything

programme.title: Utopia (AU)

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Mike surprises Frankie when he wants to be involved in his brother's crazy business scheme; Axl goes to extreme measures to prove he can handle Mike's new motorcycle; and Sue begins living in her car to avoid her horrible roommate.

programme.title: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia
programme.desc: Paddy's has been put on a list of bars that are sexually hostile to women, and the gang must attend a sexual harassment seminar to get off the list.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: Sal, Murr, Joe and Q give all you can eat buffet customers all they can handle. Also, they turn into real power tools while teaching workshops at a hardware store. Tonight's big loser suffers a punishment that will make your skin crawl.

programme.title: The Office (US)
programme.desc: Robert California stops by for the first time as CEO and leaves behind a mysterious list that has a line down the middle and every member of the office on either one side or the other. The office members frantically try to figure out what it means. Meanwhile, the new manager Andy faces a crisis.

programme.title: The Simpsons Movie
programme.desc: After Homer pollutes the town's water supply, Springfield is encased in a gigantic dome by the EPA and the Simpsons are declared fugitives.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica prepare to leave for their honeymoon as Rachel prepares to tell Ross that he's the father of her baby. While traveling, Monica and Chandler become jealous of another honeymooning couple who get bumped to first class on the airplane. Meanwhile, Joey and Phoebe scheme to get inside the newlyweds' apartment after discovering that they don't have a key.

programme.title: 30 Rock
programme.desc: Liz looks for a new best friend after discovering that Jenna has traded their friendship for fame. Tracy contemplates mortality as Dotcom and Grizz scramble to show him that life is still worth living. Meanwhile, Jack decides to end the page program in order to impress Hank Hooper.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Everyone must choose sides when a small argument between Leonard and Sheldon erupts into a heated fight during a group get-together.

programme.title: The Middle
programme.desc: Frankie asks Reverend TimTom to find out why Mike is being distant; and Sue invites Brick to visit her at college, but she loses him after running into the handsome Abercrombie guy. Meanwhile, Axl and Hutch complain to the city about an ant infestation.

programme.title: The Goldbergs (2013)
programme.desc: Adam and Brea decide to go to prom together, but when Adam worries he won't be Prom King, Beverly's actions jeopardize the event. Meanwhile, Barry teaches Erica how to be compassionate and caring when Geoff's dad is in the hospital.

programme.title: Marlon

Yvette discovers what is going on and breaks up with Demetrius after he and Marlon get into a fight at her engagement party.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie throws Ben a birthday bash but is forced to leave early for an emergency late-night filibuster. Tom tries to make some memories with his new girlfriend, Ron and Donna go hunting. April reconnects with Andy.
